source: Stellarium Sky Cultures / Chinese sky culture

constellations.name: Three Stars
constellations.myth: Three Stars, or Shenxiu, uses the bright stars western viewers know as Orion's Belt and sword. In the Chinese sky it is one of the lunar mansions and belongs to a wider courtly and seasonal map rather than a lone hunter.

constellations.name: Northern Dipper
constellations.myth: The Northern Dipper, Beidou, is a celestial ladle and regulator of time. Its turning around the pole marked seasons, directions, and imperial order in traditional Chinese astronomy.

constellations.name: Heart
constellations.myth: Heart, Xinxiu, centers on the red star Antares and forms the heart of the Azure Dragon. The star was watched as a seasonal fire, a sign of summer heat and agricultural timing.

constellations.name: Tail
constellations.myth: Tail, Weixiu, is another mansion of the Azure Dragon. The same stars that other traditions may divide into a scorpion become part of a long eastern dragon crossing the sky.

constellations.name: Wings
constellations.myth: Wings, Yixiu, belongs to the southern bird pattern. Its figure shows how Chinese astronomy often organized stars into officials, walls, beasts, and mansions rather than isolated mythic characters.
